dry_bulk.confluence_note: Spot rates are at multi-year highs and 99th percentile levels, yet qualitative fundamentals remain stubbornly negative due to weak Chinese steel demand and bloated iron ore inventories.
dry_bulk.summary: The Capesize and Panamax segments are seeing explosive momentum with 60-day rates of change over 50%, despite underlying macro concerns. High oil prices and Chinese portside iron ore stocks at 166mt (up 20% YOY) suggest the current rally may be driven by technical factors or specific trade route disruptions rather than industrial demand.
dry_bulk.outlook: Caution is advised as the gap between surging spot rates and deteriorating physical fundamentals in China suggests a potential correction.
dry_bulk.watch: China steel production figures and iron ore port inventory drawdown rates.

tanker.confluence_note: Quantitative and qualitative indicators are perfectly aligned, with Clean Tanker Z-scores exceeding +3.0 and sustained positive sentiment across all reporting categories.
tanker.summary: The tanker market is in a super-cycle phase driven by Middle East geopolitical conflict and unnavigable straits forcing longer ton-mile routes. Clean tanker rates have surged 150% over the last 60 days as global oil inventories draw down and oil prices remain elevated above $100. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2123.0 (ROC60 +150.1%), alongside dirty tankers at 2831.0 (ROC60 +78.3%).
tanker.outlook: The outlook remains exceptionally bullish as structural supply constraints and geopolitical disruptions show no immediate signs of resolution.
tanker.watch: Navigability of the Straits and OPEC production supply adjustments.

macro_note: A stark bifurcation exists between a tanker market fueled by geopolitical necessity and a dry bulk market rallying despite signs of a slowing global industrial economy and high energy costs.
